Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后举进士，登仕籍，天下仰之 - Classical expression meaning 'Later, he passed the jinshi examination, entered the ranks of officialdom, and the whole world looked up to him.' '登仕籍' refers to being officially registered as a government official.
  • 盏吾乡先辈之擅名于世者 - Grammatically and syntactically unusual phrase, possibly a title or heading indicating 'The one who was renowned in the world among my hometown’s predecessors.' '盏' may be a typo or misrendering of '赞' (praise) or '传' (transmission).
  • 以传闻异辞，不能确定其为谁 - Phrase meaning 'Due to differing accounts, it is impossible to determine who it was.' '传闻异辞' refers to conflicting or divergent reports or stories.
  • 风韵之士 - Term used to describe a man of refined taste and elegance, often with an implication of sensitivity or romantic disposition.
  • 骛于情 - Literally 'to pursue emotion,' indicating a strong inclination toward romantic or emotional experiences.
  • 注画神移 - Poetic expression meaning 'to be so entranced by the painting that his spirit is transfixed' or 'to be spiritually transported by the image.'
  • 向壁痴语 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to speak foolishly to the wall,' implying a state of delusion or obsession with the image in the painting.
  • 殆有叫活真真之想 - Phrase meaning 'almost had the wish to bring the real, living Zhenzhen to life.' '真真' is a classical term referring to a beloved woman or an idealized lover.
  • 题二绝于橙首云 - Phrase meaning 'he inscribed two quatrains at the beginning of the scroll.' '橙首' likely refers to the beginning or top of the painting scroll, possibly a misrendering or archaic term.
  • 罗縤衣裳吹不开 - Poetic line meaning 'the silk robes cannot be blown open by the wind.' This is a metaphor for the woman's grace and elegance, or perhaps a symbolic reference to a woman’s modesty or resistance to being easily understood or revealed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 进士 - A degree in the imperial examination system, representing the highest level of scholarly achievement in the Tang and Song dynasties.
  • 仕籍 - A register of government officials, indicating formal entry into the bureaucratic system.
  • 胭脂娘 - Likely the title of the story or the name of the woman at the center of the narrative. '胭脂' (yānzhī) refers to red makeup or rouge, often associated with beauty or female figures in classical Chinese literature.
  • 王氏为云林巨族 - Refers to the Wang family as a prominent clan in the town of Yunlin. '巨族' means a powerful or influential family.
  • 家畜名书古画 - Indicates that the family collected famous books and ancient paintings, which were treasured across generations.
  • 美人一辐，化工笔也 - '美人一辐' means 'one painting of a beauty,' while '化工笔也' suggests that the painting is the work of a master hand, perhaps a metaphor for divine craftsmanship.
  • 妖姬数人，倚阑扑蝶 - Describes a scene of several beautiful women leaning on a railing, chasing butterflies. This is a common motif in classical Chinese art and literature, symbolizing springtime and youthful beauty.
  • 王氏子韶，年十六 - Refers to Wang Shao, a 16-year-old son of the family, who is described as a man of refined taste and romantic disposition.
  • 题二绝于橙首云 - '二绝' refers to two quatrains, a common poetic form in classical Chinese poetry. '橙首' may be a misrendering of '题首' (at the beginning of the scroll) or '橙首' could be a specific term related to the painting’s structure.
  • 二八王郎题赠美人 - '二八' is a classical way of saying 16, so '二八王郎' means 'the 16-year-old Wang youth.' The phrase '题赠美人' means 'inscribed as a gift to the beauty.'
  • 诸姊妹一粲 - Refers to the sisters laughing or smiling at the poem, indicating approval or amusement.
  • 父见之而晒 - The father sees the poem and smiles, perhaps with amusement or disapproval. '晒' here is likely a variant of '哂' (to smile slightly), indicating a subtle reaction.
  • 取藏之，韶不敢问 - The father takes the poem and stores it, and Wang Shao dares not ask about it, suggesting the father may be hiding or concealing something.
  • 家稍落 - The family’s status declines slightly, perhaps due to the father’s death or other financial difficulties.
  • 舌耕于他姓 - A metaphor for working as a teacher or scholar for another family, often used to describe a scholar who supports himself by teaching.
  • 族子无赖 - A relative who is a 'wretch' or 'rascal,' indicating a morally or socially disreputable person.
  • 尽窃其家书画卖之 - The relative steals all the family’s paintings and calligraphy and sells them, indicating the family’s decline and loss.
  • 美人图卷，亦未知流落谁手 - The painting of the beauty is now unknown to be in whose possession, indicating it has been lost or dispersed.
  • 韶嗒然懊恨，如丧拱壁 - Wang Shao is deeply distressed and feels as if he has lost a treasure. '拱壁' is a metaphor for a valuable object, often used to describe a rare or precious item.

  • 滕王 - A reference to Teng Wang, a historical figure, likely Teng Wang of the Tang dynasty, known for his literary and political achievements. The reference to '滕王尺五团' may be a poetic allusion to the *Tengwang Pavilion* (滕王阁), a famous pavilion in Nanchang, Jiangxi, associated with the *Tengwang Pavilion Preface* by Wang Bo.
  • 楚阳台 - A reference to the *Chu Yangtai*, a legendary place in Chinese mythology associated with the romantic tale of Xiang Yu and his wife, or more generally with a place of love and longing.

  • 甚可憎畏。忽以手探面，则皮壳顿落，类今梨园中面具，以付其从者收之，则翩翩然美丈夫矣，而年亦少，与姑龄上下。 - 甚可憎畏 means 'very terrifying.' 忽以手探面 means 'suddenly touched his face.' 皮壳顿落 means 'the skin fell off.' 类今梨园中面具 compares the skin to a mask used in the theater. 以付其从者收之 means 'gave it to his attendant to collect.' 翩翩然美丈夫矣 means 'became a handsome man.' 而年亦少 means 'and was also young.' 与姑龄上下 means 'about the same age as the woman.'
  • 问姑曰：“其人来否”姑曰：“来矣。”呼王出见之。 - 问姑曰 means 'asked the woman.' 其人来否 means 'whether that person has come.' 来矣 means 'he has come.' 呼王出见之 means 'called Wang out to meet him.'
  • 戚谊其殷勤，不啻王导之待何充也。 - 戚谊其殷勤 means 'their relationship was very cordial.' 不啻王导之待何充也 is a comparison to the close relationship between Wang Dao and He Chong.
  • 少顷，设饮食，酒馔丰备。王素不胜杯杓，姑强之，王勉为尽觞焉。 - 少顷 means 'a short while later.' 设饮食 means 'arranged food and drink.' 酒馔丰备 means 'the wine and food were plentiful.' 王素不胜杯杓 means 'Wang usually couldn't drink much.' 姑强之 means 'the woman forced him.' 王勉为尽觞焉 means 'Wang reluctantly finished his cup.'
  • 旋有书吏呈一牒，令共观之，内书王姓名籍贯，胪列其科笫及事行，体例似年谱。 - 旋有书吏呈一牒 means 'soon a scribe presented a document.' 令共观之 means 'ordered them to look at it together.' 内书王姓名籍贯 means 'the document listed Wang's name, origin, and family background.' 胪列其科笫及事行 means 'listed his achievements and deeds.' 体例似年谱 means 'the format was similar to an annual chronicle.'
  • 有一页大书六字曰：“承宣布政使司”。 - 大书六字 means 'wrote six large characters.' 承宣布政使司 is the title of a high-ranking official in the Ming dynasty.
  • 姑丈取笔，下注一酒字。审阅未尽数页，遽付吏持去藏之。不令见。 - 取笔，下注一酒字 means 'took a pen and wrote the character for 'wine' below.' 审阅未尽数页 means 'reviewed only a few pages.' 遽付吏持去藏之 means 'quickly gave it to the scribe to take away and hide it.' 不令见 means 'did not allow it to be seen.'
  • 酒罢，引之就寝，帷榻茵席亦华焕非常。 - 酒罢 means 'after the wine was finished.' 引之就寝 means 'led him to bed.' 帷榻茵席亦华焕非常 means 'the curtains, bed, and bedding were also very luxurious.'
